description: Unify macOS and Windows desktop updates under one control-plane policy model with a native-updater-first execution path and installer fallback.

userStories.title: Standardize desktop release metadata for updater-first targets
userStories.description: As a release pipeline, I want macOS and Windows targets to publish the same installer, updater, signature, and policy metadata shape so that runtime update logic can stay platform-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: Formal desktop release metadata stores installer, updater, signature, and release policy for both darwin and windows targets
Target manifest generation keeps installer artifact_url while embedding updater url and signature when signed updater artifacts exist
Release metadata remains keyed by platform and arch without introducing platform-specific schema branches
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Tighten publish and doctor validation for signed updater artifacts
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want publish validation to catch incomplete updater bundles before release so that updater-first rollout does not ship broken manifests.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop publish validation fails when updater and signature are not both present for a target expected to ship signed native updater artifacts
Release doctor output explicitly reports missing updater or signature files per platform and arch
Windows and macOS validation rules are driven from one shared updater expectation instead of divergent ad hoc checks
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Preserve unified policy payloads on update hint and updater routes
userStories.description: As a desktop client, I want update-hint and updater responses to carry the same enforcement semantics on both platforms so that the shell can decide blocking behavior without platform-specific policy branches.
acceptanceCriteria: Update hint payload includes latestVersion, mandatory, enforcementState, blockNewRuns, reasonCode, reasonMessage, manifestUrl, and artifactUrl
Native updater route includes mandatory, enforcement_state, block_new_runs, reason_code, reason_message, and external_download_url alongside version, url, and signature
Managed release resolution uses the same policy source for macOS and Windows requests
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Switch desktop upgrade execution to updater-first with installer fallback
userStories.description: As a desktop user, I want both macOS and Windows to try the signed native updater first and fall back to the installer only when native update is unavailable or fails.
acceptanceCriteria: executeDesktopUpdateUpgrade attempts checkDesktopUpdate before any installer launch on both macOS and Windows Tauri runtimes
A supported and available signed native update triggers downloadAndInstallDesktopUpdate
When native updater is unsupported, returns no update payload, or install start fails, the flow resolves installer artifactUrl and falls back to downloadAndLaunchDesktopInstaller
Non-Tauri or browser contexts continue to open the external installer or manifest URL
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Persist upgrade scene snapshots for both native and installer paths
userStories.description: As a desktop user, I want the app to restore my pre-upgrade page after either native updater or installer-driven upgrades so that forced updates do not drop my working context.
acceptanceCriteria: The desktop shell writes a scene snapshot before starting either native updater installation or installer fallback
Scene snapshot records targetVersion, primaryView, and overlayView for forced-update restore
On restart, matching targetVersion restores the saved scene and clears the snapshot
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Unify desktop update progress and blocking UI across platforms
userStories.description: As a desktop user, I want one consistent upgrade experience regardless of platform so that recommended updates, deferred forced updates, and restart-ready states behave the same on macOS and Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop update progress messaging covers native download, installer fallback, installer started, and ready-to-restart states without platform-specific copy branches
DesktopUpdateGuard continues to honor recommended, required_after_run, and required_now enforcement states after the updater-first rollout
Settings or in-app update surfaces reflect the active action state during native updater and installer fallback flows
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Cover updater-first rollout with desktop update regression tests
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want automated tests for the unified updater-first flow so that future changes do not silently break fallback, enforcement, or restart behavior on one platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop upgrade tests cover macOS native updater success and Windows native updater success
Desktop upgrade tests cover fallback to installer when native updater is unsupported or install start fails
Control-plane tests cover updater payload and hint payload policy fields for managed desktop releases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
